message.content:
You are a senior security auditor reviewing the chia-blockchain node for its bug-bounty program. Rigorous, skeptical, concrete data-flow required. An earlier pass over daemon/server/rpc found nothing easy — go deeper.
REPO ROOT: C:/bugBounty/targets/chia-blockchain. PRIMARY DIR: C:/bugBounty/targets/chia-blockchain/chia/full_node (read relevant .py in full, recurse). Use Grep/Glob to follow handlers, decorators (@api_request), call chains, and the protocol message definitions in chia/protocols/*.

THREAT MODEL (chia full node / wallet):
- HIGHEST value: an UNTRUSTED remote P2P peer sending crafted protocol messages to a full node (no auth; anyone can connect). Goal classes: remote DoS (unbounded alloc/CPU, decompression, infinite loop), consensus/validation bypass, memory/logic errors, peer-data injection.
- RPC clients: the wallet/full_node RPC and the daemon websocket. Sometimes reachable beyond localhost or cross-origin. Goal: missing/weak auth or origin checks, SSRF, parameter injection, command injection, key/secret disclosure.
- Wallet users / counterparties: offer/trade files and CATs/NFTs from other users. Goal: offer manipulation enabling fund theft, signature/spend-bundle validation gaps, integer/amount handling.
- data_layer HTTP server/clients. Goal: path traversal, SSRF, auth bypass, arbitrary file read/write.
Prefer findings reachable by a remote/untrusted actor with no or low privilege.

CLUSTER FOCUS: Mempool + consensus validation. Read mempool_manager.py, mempool.py, and pull in chia/consensus/*.py (block_body_validation, blockchain, cost/fee logic). Hunt: spend-bundle/transaction validation gaps, fee/cost miscalculation, double-spend or replacement bugs, consensus rule that a crafted block/tx can bypass, integer overflow in amounts/cost.
CLASSES: consensus/validation bypass, double-spend, fee/cost manipulation, integer overflow

METHOD: find the dangerous operations; trace BACKWARD to an untrusted source (peer message field, RPC param, offer file, HTTP request); check for validation/limits/auth/mitigations in between; only report when a remote/low-priv actor can reach it. Severity = impact x reachability (remote unauth = highest).
At most 6 findings, best-first. Zero is an acceptable honest answer. Each: exact file:line, source, sink, concrete data_flow with function names, preconditions, exploitability, honest confidence in [0,1]. Return ONLY the structured object.

79	def compute_assert_height(
80	    removal_coin_records: dict[bytes32, CoinRecord],
81	    conds: SpendBundleConditions,
82	) -> TimelockConditions:
83	    """
84	    Computes the most restrictive height- and seconds assertion in the spend bundle.
85	    Relative heights and times are resolved using the confirmed heights and
86	    timestamps from the coin records.
87	    """
88	
89	    ret = TimelockConditions()
90	    ret.assert_height = uint32(conds.height_absolute)
91	    ret.assert_seconds = uint64(conds.seconds_absolute)
92	    ret.assert_before_height = (
93	        uint32(conds.before_height_absolute) if conds.before_height_absolute is not None else None
94	    )
95	    ret.assert_before_seconds = (
96	        uint64(conds.before_seconds_absolute) if conds.before_seconds_absolute is not None else None
97	    )
98	
99	    for spend in conds.spends:
100	        if spend.height_relative is not None:
101	            h = uint32(removal_coin_records[bytes32(spend.coin_id)].confirmed_block_index + spend.height_relative)
102	            ret.assert_height = max(ret.assert_height, h)

104	        if spend.seconds_relative is not None:

141	def is_atom_canonical(clvm_buffer: bytes, offset: int) -> tuple[int, bool]:
142	    b = clvm_buffer[offset]
143	    if (b & 0b11000000) == 0b10000000:
144	        # 6 bits length prefix
145	        mask = 0b00111111
146	        prefix_len = 0
147	        min_value = 1
148	    elif (b & 0b11100000) == 0b11000000:
149	        # 5 + 8 bits length prefix
150	        mask = 0b00011111
151	        prefix_len = 1
152	        min_value = 1 << 6
153	    elif (b & 0b11110000) == 0b11100000:
154	        # 4 + 8 + 8 bits length prefix
155	        mask = 0b00001111
156	        prefix_len = 2
157	        min_value = 1 << (5 + 8)
158	    elif (b & 0b11111000) == 0b11110000:
159	        # 3 + 8 + 8 + 8 bits length prefix
160	        mask = 0b00000111
161	        prefix_len = 3
162	        min_value = 1 << (4 + 8 + 8)
163	    elif (b & 0b11111100) == 0b11111000:
164	        # 2 + 8 + 8 + 8 + 8 bits length prefix
165	        mask = 0b00000011
166	        prefix_len = 4
167	        min_value = 1 << (3 + 8 + 8 + 8)
168	    elif (b & 0b11111110) == 0b11111100:
169	        # 1 + 8 + 8 + 8 + 8 + 8 bits length prefix
170	        mask = 0b00000001
171	        prefix_len = 5
172	        min_value = 1 << (2 + 8 + 8 + 8 + 8)
173	
174	    atom_len = b & mask
175	    for i in range(prefix_len):
176	        atom_len <<= 8
177	        offset += 1
178	        atom_len |= clvm_buffer[offset]
179	
180	    return 1 + prefix_len + atom_len, atom_len >= min_value

183	def is_clvm_canonical(clvm_buffer: bytes) -> bool:
184	    """
185	    checks whether the CLVM serialization is all canonical representation.
186	    atoms can be serialized in more than one way by using more bytes than
187	    necessary to encode the length prefix. This functions ensures that all atoms are
188	    encoded with the shortest representation. back-references are not allowed
189	    and will make this function return false
190	    """
191	    assert clvm_buffer != b""
192	
193	    offset = 0
194	    tokens_left = 1
195	    while True:
196	        b = clvm_buffer[offset]
197	
198	        # pair
199	        if b == 0xFF:
200	            tokens_left += 1
201	            offset += 1
202	            continue
203	
204	        # back references cannot be considered canonical, since they may be
205	        # encoded in many different ways
206	        if b == 0xFE:
207	            return False
208	
209	        # small atom or NIL
210	        if b <= 0x80:
211	            tokens_left -= 1
212	            offset += 1
213	        else:
214	            atom_len, canonical = is_atom_canonical(clvm_buffer, offset)
215	            if not canonical:
216	                return False
217	            tokens_left -= 1
218	            offset += atom_len
219	
220	        if tokens_left == 0:
221	            break
222	
223	    # if there's garbage at the end, it's not canonical
224	    return offset == len(clvm_buffer)

227	def check_removals(
228	    removals: dict[bytes32, CoinRecord],
229	    bundle_coin_spends: dict[bytes32, BundleCoinSpend],
230	    *,
231	    get_items_by_coin_ids: Callable[[list[bytes32]], list[MempoolItem]],
232	) -> tuple[Err | None, list[MempoolItem]]:
233	    """
234	    This function checks for double spends, unknown spends and conflicting transactions in mempool.
235	    Returns Error (if any), the set of existing MempoolItems with conflicting spends (if any).
236	    Note that additions are not checked for duplicates, because having duplicate additions requires also
237	    having duplicate removals.
238	    """
239	    conflicts = set()
240	    for coin_id, coin_bcs in bundle_coin_spends.items():
241	        # 1. Checks if it's been spent already
242	        if removals[coin_id].spent and not coin_bcs.supports_fast_forward:
243	            return Err.DOUBLE_SPEND, []
244	
245	        # 2. Checks if there's a mempool conflict
246	        conflicting_items = get_items_by_coin_ids([coin_id])
247	        for item in conflicting_items:
248	            if item in conflicts:
249	                continue
250	            conflict_bcs = item.bundle_coin_spends.get(coin_id)
251	            if conflict_bcs is None:
252	                # Check if this is an item that spends an older ff singleton
253	                # version with a latest version that matches our coin ID.
254	                conflict_bcs = next(
255	                    (
256	                        bcs
257	                        for bcs in item.bundle_coin_spends.values()
258	                        if bcs.latest_singleton_lineage is not None and bcs.latest_singleton_lineage.coin_id == coin_id
259	                    ),
260	                    None,
261	                )
262	                # We're not expected to get here but let's handle it gracefully
263	                if conflict_bcs is None:
264	                    log.warning(f"Coin ID {coin_id} expected but not found in mempool item {item.name}")
265	                    return Err.INVALID_SPEND_BUNDLE, []
266	            # if the spend we're adding to the mempool is not DEDUP nor FF, it's
267	            # just a regular conflict
268	            if not coin_bcs.supports_fast_forward and not coin_bcs.eligible_for_dedup:
269	                conflicts.add(item)
270	
271	            # if the spend we're adding is FF, but there's a conflicting spend
272	            # that isn't FF, they can't be chained, so that's a conflict
273	            elif coin_bcs.supports_fast_forward and not conflict_bcs.supports_fast_forward:
274	                conflicts.add(item)
275	
276	            # if the spend we're adding is DEDUP, but there's a conflicting spend
277	            # that isn't DEDUP, we cannot merge them, so that's a conflict
278	            elif coin_bcs.eligible_for_dedup and not conflict_bcs.eligible_for_dedup:
279	                conflicts.add(item)
280	
281	            # if the spend we're adding is DEDUP but the existing spend has a
282	            # different solution, we cannot merge them, so that's a conflict
283	            elif coin_bcs.eligible_for_dedup and bytes(coin_bcs.coin_spend.solution) != bytes(
284	                conflict_bcs.coin_spend.solution
285	            ):
286	                conflicts.add(item)
287	
288	    if len(conflicts) > 0:
289	        return Err.MEMPOOL_CONFLICT, list(conflicts)
290	    return None, []

437	        all_transactions: set[bytes32] = set()
438	        byte_array_list = []
439	        for key in self.mempool.all_item_ids():
440	            if key not in all_transactions:

447	    def is_fee_enough(self, fees: uint64, cost: uint64) -> bool:
448	        """
449	        Determines whether any of the pools can accept a transaction with a given fees
450	        and cost.
451	        """
452	        if cost == 0:
453	            return False
454	        fees_per_cost = fees / cost
455	        if not self.mempool.at_full_capacity(cost):
456	            return True
457	        if fees_per_cost < self.nonzero_fee_minimum_fpc:
458	            return False
459	        min_fee_rate = self.mempool.get_min_fee_rate(cost)
460	        return min_fee_rate is not None and fees_per_cost > min_fee_rate

485	    async def pre_validate_spendbundle(
486	        self,
487	        spend_bundle: SpendBundle,
488	        spend_bundle_id: bytes32 | None = None,
489	        bls_cache: BLSCache | None = None,
490	        *,
491	        fee_per_cost: float = 0.0,
492	    ) -> SpendBundleConditions:
493	        """
494	        Errors are included within the cached_result.
495	        This runs in another process so we don't block the main thread
496	        """
497	
498	        if spend_bundle.coin_spends == []:
499	            raise ValidationError(Err.INVALID_SPEND_BUNDLE, "Empty SpendBundle")
500	
501	        assert self.peak is not None
502	
503	        self._worker_queue_size += 1
504	        try:
505	            flags = get_flags_for_height_and_constants(self.peak.height, self.constants)
506	            sbc: SpendBundleConditions
507	            sbc, new_cache_entries, duration = await self.pool.run_in_loop(
508	                validate_clvm_and_signature,
509	                spend_bundle,
510	                self.max_tx_clvm_cost,
511	                self.constants,
512	                flags | MEMPOOL_MODE,
513	                nice=(5, -fee_per_cost),
514	            )
515	        # validate_clvm_and_signature raises a ValueError with an error code
516	        except ValueError as e:
517	            # Convert that to a ValidationError
518	            if len(e.args) > 1:
519	                error = Err(e.args[1])
520	                raise ValidationError(error)
521	            else:
522	                raise ValidationError(Err.UNKNOWN)  # pragma: no cover
523	        finally:
524	            self._worker_queue_size -= 1
525	
526	        if sbc.num_atoms > sbc.cost * 60_000_000 / self.constants.MAX_BLOCK_COST_CLVM:
527	            raise ValueError("too many atoms")
528	
529	        if sbc.num_pairs > sbc.cost * 60_000_000 / self.constants.MAX_BLOCK_COST_CLVM:
530	            raise ValueError("too many pairs")
531	
532	        if duration > self.validation_timeout:
533	            raise ValueError(f"timeout {duration:0.4} s")
534	
535	        cost = sbc.execution_cost + sbc.condition_cost
536	        if cost == 0 or (duration > 0.1 and duration * 1e9 / cost > self.validation_timeout * 5.0):
537	            raise ValueError(f"timeout ({duration * 1e9 / cost:0.4} ns/cost)")
538	
539	        if bls_cache is not None:
540	            bls_cache.update(new_cache_entries)
541	
542	        if spend_bundle_id is None:
543	            spend_bundle_id = spend_bundle.name()
544	
545	        log.log(
546	            logging.DEBUG if duration < self.validation_timeout else logging.WARNING,
547	            f"pre_validate_spendbundle took {duration:0.4f} seconds {duration * 1e9 / cost:0.4} ns/cost "
548	            f"for {spend_bundle_id} (queue-size: {self._worker_queue_size})",
549	        )
550	        return sbc

552	    async def add_spend_bundle(
553	        self,
554	        new_spend: SpendBundle,
555	        conds: SpendBundleConditions,
556	        spend_name: bytes32,
557	        first_added_height: uint32,
558	        get_coin_records: Callable[[Collection[bytes32]], Awaitable[list[CoinRecord]]] | None = None,
559	        get_unspent_lineage_info_for_puzzle_hash: Callable[[bytes32], Awaitable[UnspentLineageInfo | None]]
560	        | None = None,
561	    ) -> SpendBundleAddInfo:
562	        """
563	        Validates and adds to mempool a new_spend with the given
564	        SpendBundleConditions, and spend_name, and the current mempool. The mempool
565	        should be locked during this call (blockchain lock). If there are mempool
566	        conflicts, the conflicting spends might be removed (if the new spend is
567	        a superset of the previous). Otherwise, the new spend might be
568	        added to the potential pool.
569	
570	        Args:
571	            new_spend: spend bundle to validate and add
572	            conds: SpendBundleConditions resulting from running the clvm in the spend bundle's coin spends
573	            spend_name: hash of the spend bundle data, passed in as an optimization
574	
575	        Returns:
576	            Optional[uint64]: cost of the entire transaction, None iff status is FAILED
577	            MempoolInclusionStatus:  SUCCESS (should add to pool), FAILED (cannot add), and PENDING (can add later)
578	            list[MempoolRemoveInfo]: conflicting mempool items which were removed, if no Err
579	            Optional[Err]: Err is set iff status is FAILED
580	        """
581	
582	        # Skip if already added
583	        existing_item = self.mempool.get_item_by_id(spend_name)
584	        if existing_item is not None:
585	            return SpendBundleAddInfo(existing_item.cost, MempoolInclusionStatus.SUCCESS, [], None)
586	
587	        if get_coin_records is None:
588	            get_coin_records = self.get_coin_records
589	
590	        if get_unspent_lineage_info_for_puzzle_hash is None:
591	            get_unspent_lineage_info_for_puzzle_hash = self.get_unspent_lineage_info_for_puzzle_hash
592	
593	        err, item, remove_items = await self.validate_spend_bundle(
594	            new_spend,
595	            conds,
596	            spend_name,
597	            first_added_height,
598	            get_coin_records,
599	            get_unspent_lineage_info_for_puzzle_hash,
600	        )
601	        if err is None:
602	            # No error, immediately add to mempool, after removing conflicting TXs.
603	            assert item is not None
604	            conflict = self.mempool.remove_from_pool(remove_items, MempoolRemoveReason.CONFLICT)
605	            info = self.mempool.add_to_pool(item)
606	            if info.error is not None:
607	                return SpendBundleAddInfo(item.cost, MempoolInclusionStatus.FAILED, [], info.error)
608	            return SpendBundleAddInfo(item.cost, MempoolInclusionStatus.SUCCESS, [*info.removals, conflict], None)
609	        elif err is Err.MEMPOOL_CONFLICT and item is not None:
610	            # The transaction has a conflict with another item in the
611	            # mempool, put it aside and re-try it later
612	            self._conflict_cache.add(item)
613	            return SpendBundleAddInfo(item.cost, MempoolInclusionStatus.PENDING, [], err)
614	        elif item is not None:
615	            # The transasction has a height assertion and is not yet valid.
616	            # remember it to try it again later
617	            self._pending_cache.add(item)
618	            return SpendBundleAddInfo(item.cost, MempoolInclusionStatus.PENDING, [], err)
619	        else:
620	            # Cannot add to the mempool or pending pool.
621	            return SpendBundleAddInfo(None, MempoolInclusionStatus.FAILED, [], err)
622	
623	    async def validate_spend_bundle(
624	        self,
625	        new_spend: SpendBundle,
626	        conds: SpendBundleConditions,
627	        spend_name: bytes32,
628	        first_added_height: uint32,
629	        get_coin_records: Callable[[Collection[bytes32]], Awaitable[list[CoinRecord]]],
630	        get_unspent_lineage_info_for_puzzle_hash: Callable[[bytes32], Awaitable[UnspentLineageInfo | None]],
631	    ) -> tuple[Err | None, MempoolItem | None, list[bytes32]]:
632	        """
633	        Validates new_spend with the given SpendBundleConditions, and
634	        spend_name, and the current mempool. The mempool should
635	        be locked during this call (blockchain lock).
636	
637	        Args:
638	            new_spend: spend bundle to validate
639	            conds: result of running the clvm transaction
640	            spend_name: hash of the spend bundle data, passed in as an optimization
641	            first_added_height: The block height that `new_spend`  first entered this node's mempool.
642	                Used to estimate how long a spend has taken to be included on the chain.
643	                This value could differ node to node. Not preserved across full_node restarts.
644	
645	        Returns:
646	            Optional[Err]: Err is set if we cannot add to the mempool, None if we will immediately add to mempool
647	            Optional[MempoolItem]: the item to add (to mempool or pending pool)
648	            list[bytes32]: conflicting mempool items to remove, if no Err
649	        """
650	        start_time = time.monotonic()
651	        if self.peak is None:
652	            return Err.MEMPOOL_NOT_INITIALIZED, None, []
653	
654	        cost = conds.cost
655	
656	        removal_names: set[bytes32] = set()
657	        additions_dict: dict[bytes32, Coin] = {}
658	        addition_amount: int = 0
659	
660	        # Map of coin ID to SpendConditions
661	        spend_conditions = {bytes32(spend.coin_id): spend for spend in conds.spends}
662	
663	        # if this happens, the SpendBundle doesn't match the
664	        # SpendBundleConditions.
665	        assert len(new_spend.coin_spends) == len(spend_conditions)
666	
667	        bundle_coin_spends: dict[bytes32, BundleCoinSpend] = {}
668	        for coin_spend in new_spend.coin_spends:
669	            coin_id = coin_spend.coin.name()
670	            removal_names.add(coin_id)
671	
672	            # if this coin_id isn't found, the SpendBundle doesn't match the
673	            # SpendBundleConditions.
674	            spend_conds = spend_conditions.pop(coin_id)
675	
676	            if bool(spend_conds.flags & ELIGIBLE_FOR_DEDUP) and not is_clvm_canonical(bytes(coin_spend.solution)):
677	                return Err.INVALID_COIN_SOLUTION, None, []
678	
679	            lineage_info = None
680	            if bool(spend_conds.flags & ELIGIBLE_FOR_FF) and supports_fast_forward(coin_spend):
681	                # Make sure the fast forward spend still has a version that is
682	                # still unspent, because if the singleton has been spent in a
683	                # non-FF spend, this fast forward spend will never become valid.
684	                # So treat this as a normal spend, which requires the exact coin
685	                # to exist and be unspent.
686	                # Singletons that were created before the optimization of using
687	                # spent_index will also fail this test, and such spends will
688	                # fall back to be treated as non-FF spends.
689	                lineage_info = await get_unspent_lineage_info_for_puzzle_hash(spend_conds.puzzle_hash)
690	
691	            spend_additions = []
692	            for puzzle_hash, amount, _ in spend_conds.create_coin:
693	                child_coin = Coin(coin_id, puzzle_hash, uint64(amount))
694	                spend_additions.append(child_coin)
695	                additions_dict[child_coin.name()] = child_coin
696	                addition_amount += amount
697	
698	            bundle_coin_spends[coin_id] = BundleCoinSpend(
699	                coin_spend=coin_spend,
700	                eligible_for_dedup=bool(spend_conds.flags & ELIGIBLE_FOR_DEDUP),
701	                additions=spend_additions,
702	                cost=uint64(spend_conds.condition_cost + spend_conds.execution_cost),
703	                latest_singleton_lineage=lineage_info,
704	            )
705	
706	        # fast forward spends are only allowed when bundled with other, non-FF, spends
707	        # in order to evict an FF spend, it must be associated with a normal
708	        # spend that can be included in a block or invalidated some other way
709	        if all([s.supports_fast_forward for s in bundle_coin_spends.values()]):
710	            return Err.INVALID_SPEND_BUNDLE, None, []
711	
712	        removal_record_dict: dict[bytes32, CoinRecord] = {}
713	        removal_amount: int = 0
714	        removal_records = await get_coin_records(removal_names)
715	        for record in removal_records:
716	            removal_record_dict[record.coin.name()] = record
717	
718	        for name in removal_names:
719	            if name not in removal_record_dict and name not in additions_dict:
720	                return Err.UNKNOWN_UNSPENT, None, []
721	            if name in additions_dict:
722	                removal_coin = additions_dict[name]
723	                # The timestamp and block-height of this coin being spent needs
724	                # to be consistent with what we use to check time-lock
725	                # conditions (below). All spends (including ephemeral coins) are
726	                # spent simultaneously. Ephemeral coins with an
727	                # ASSERT_SECONDS_RELATIVE 0 condition are still OK to spend in
728	                # the same block.
729	                assert self.peak.timestamp is not None
730	                removal_record = CoinRecord(
731	                    removal_coin,
732	                    uint32(self.peak.height + 1),
733	                    uint32(0),
734	                    False,
735	                    self.peak.timestamp,
736	                )
737	                removal_record_dict[name] = removal_record
738	            else:
739	                removal_record = removal_record_dict[name]
740	            removal_amount += removal_record.coin.amount
741	
742	        fees = uint64(removal_amount - addition_amount)
743	
744	        if cost == 0:
745	            return Err.UNKNOWN, None, []
746	
747	        if cost > self.max_tx_clvm_cost:
748	            return Err.BLOCK_COST_EXCEEDS_MAX, None, []
749	
750	        # this is not very likely to happen, but it's here to ensure SQLite
751	        # never runs out of precision in its computation of fees.
752	        # sqlite's integers are signed int64, so the max value they can
753	        # represent is 2^63-1
754	        if fees > MEMPOOL_ITEM_FEE_LIMIT or SQLITE_INT_MAX - self.mempool.total_mempool_fees() <= fees:
755	            return Err.INVALID_BLOCK_FEE_AMOUNT, None, []
756	
757	        fees_per_cost: float = fees / cost
758	        # If pool is at capacity check the fee, if not then accept even without the fee
759	        if self.mempool.at_full_capacity(cost):
760	            if fees_per_cost < self.nonzero_fee_minimum_fpc:
761	                return Err.INVALID_FEE_TOO_CLOSE_TO_ZERO, None, []
762	            min_fee_rate = self.mempool.get_min_fee_rate(cost)
763	            if min_fee_rate is None:
764	                return Err.INVALID_COST_RESULT, None, []
765	            if fees_per_cost <= min_fee_rate:
766	                return Err.INVALID_FEE_LOW_FEE, None, []
767	
768	        # Check removals against UnspentDB + DiffStore + Mempool + SpendBundle
769	        # Use this information later when constructing a block
770	        fail_reason, conflicts = check_removals(
771	            removal_record_dict, bundle_coin_spends, get_items_by_coin_ids=self.mempool.get_items_by_coin_ids
772	        )
773	
774	        # If we have a mempool conflict, continue, since we still want to keep around the TX in the pending pool.
775	        if fail_reason is not None and fail_reason is not Err.MEMPOOL_CONFLICT:
776	            return fail_reason, None, []
777	
778	        # Verify conditions, create hash_key list for aggsig check
779	        for spend in conds.spends:
780	            coin_record: CoinRecord = removal_record_dict[bytes32(spend.coin_id)]
781	            # Check that the revealed removal puzzles actually match the puzzle hash
782	            if spend.puzzle_hash != coin_record.coin.puzzle_hash:
783	                log.warning("Mempool rejecting transaction because of wrong puzzle_hash")
784	                log.warning(f"{spend.puzzle_hash.hex()} != {coin_record.coin.puzzle_hash.hex()}")
785	                return Err.WRONG_PUZZLE_HASH, None, []
786	
787	        # the height and time we pass in here represent the previous transaction
788	        # block's height and timestamp. In the mempool, the most recent peak
789	        # block we've received will be the previous transaction block, from the
790	        # point-of-view of the next block to be farmed. Therefore we pass in the
791	        # current peak's height and timestamp
792	        assert self.peak.timestamp is not None
793	        tl_error_rust: int | None = check_time_locks(
794	            removal_record_dict,
795	            conds,
796	            self.peak.height,
797	            self.peak.timestamp,
798	            nowrap=self.peak.height >= self.constants.HARD_FORK2_HEIGHT,
799	        )
800	        tl_error: Err | None = None
801	        if tl_error_rust is not None:
802	            tl_error = Err(tl_error_rust)
803	
804	        timelocks: TimelockConditions = compute_assert_height(removal_record_dict, conds)
805	
806	        if timelocks.assert_before_height is not None and timelocks.assert_before_height <= timelocks.assert_height:
807	            # returning None as the "potential" means it failed. We won't store it
808	            # in the pending cache
809	            return Err.IMPOSSIBLE_HEIGHT_ABSOLUTE_CONSTRAINTS, None, []  # MempoolInclusionStatus.FAILED
810	        if timelocks.assert_before_seconds is not None and timelocks.assert_before_seconds <= timelocks.assert_seconds:
811	            return Err.IMPOSSIBLE_SECONDS_ABSOLUTE_CONSTRAINTS, None, []  # MempoolInclusionStatus.FAILED
812	
813	        potential = MempoolItem(
814	            new_spend.aggregated_signature,
815	            uint64(fees),
816	            conds,
817	            spend_name,
818	            first_added_height,
819	            timelocks.assert_height,
820	            timelocks.assert_before_height,
821	            timelocks.assert_before_seconds,
822	            bundle_coin_spends,
823	        )
824	
825	        if tl_error:
826	            if tl_error is Err.ASSERT_HEIGHT_ABSOLUTE_FAILED or tl_error is Err.ASSERT_HEIGHT_RELATIVE_FAILED:
827	                return tl_error, potential, []  # MempoolInclusionStatus.PENDING
828	            else:
829	                return tl_error, None, []  # MempoolInclusionStatus.FAILED
830	
831	        if fail_reason is Err.MEMPOOL_CONFLICT:
832	            log.debug(f"Replace attempted. number of MempoolItems: {len(conflicts)}")
833	            if not can_replace(conflicts, potential):
834	                return Err.MEMPOOL_CONFLICT, potential, []
835	
836	        duration = time.monotonic() - start_time
837	
838	        log.log(
839	            logging.DEBUG if duration < self.validation_timeout else logging.WARNING,
840	            f"add_spendbundle {spend_name} took {duration:0.2f} seconds. "
841	            f"Cost: {cost} ({round(100.0 * cost / self.constants.MAX_BLOCK_COST_CLVM, 3)}% of max block cost)",
842	        )
843	
844	        if duration > self.validation_timeout:
845	            log.warning("validating spend took too long, rejecting")
846	            return Err.INVALID_SPEND_BUNDLE, None, []
847	
848	        return None, potential, [item.name for item in conflicts]
849	
850	    def get_spendbundle(self, bundle_hash: bytes32) -> SpendBundle | None:
851	        """Returns a full SpendBundle if it's inside one the mempools"""
852	        item: MempoolItem | None = self.mempool.get_item_by_id(bundle_hash)
853	        if item is not None:
854	            return item.to_spend_bundle()
855	        return None
856	
857	    def get_mempool_item(self, bundle_hash: bytes32, include_pending: bool = False) -> MempoolItem | None:
858	        """
859	        Returns the MempoolItem in the mempool that matches the provided spend bundle hash (id)
860	        or None if not found.
861	
862	        If include_pending is specified, also check the PENDING cache.
863	        """
864	        item = self.mempool.get_item_by_id(bundle_hash)

1098	    This function implements the mempool replacement rules. Given a Mempool item
1099	    we're attempting to insert into the mempool (new_item) and the set of existing
1100	    mempool items that conflict with it, this function answers the question whether
1101	    the existing items can be replaced by the new one.
1102	    """
1103	
1104	    conflicting_fees = 0
1105	    conflicting_cost = 0
1106	    assert_height: uint32 | None = None
1107	    assert_before_height: uint32 | None = None
1108	    assert_before_seconds: uint64 | None = None

1110	    # eligibility for dedup and fast-forward. Doing so could be abused by
1111	    # denying such spends from operating as intended
1112	    # collect all coins that are eligible for dedup and FF in the existing items
1113	    existing_ff_spends: set[bytes32] = set()
1114	    existing_dedup_spends: set[bytes32] = set()
1115	
1116	    for item in conflicting_items:
1117	        conflicting_fees += item.fee
1118	        conflicting_cost += item.cost
1119	
1120	        # All coins spent in all conflicting items must also be spent in the new item. (superset rule). This is
1121	        # important because otherwise there exists an attack. A user spends coin A. An attacker replaces the
1122	        # bundle with AB with a higher fee. An attacker then replaces the bundle with just B with a higher
1123	        # fee than AB therefore kicking out A altogether. The better way to solve this would be to keep a cache
1124	        # of booted transactions like A, and retry them after they get removed from mempool due to a conflict.
1125	        for coin_id, bcs in item.bundle_coin_spends.items():

1127	                log.debug("Rejecting conflicting tx as it does not spend conflicting coin %s", coin_id)

1131	            if bcs.eligible_for_dedup:
1132	                existing_dedup_spends.add(bytes32(coin_id))

1135	        assert_before_height = optional_min(assert_before_height, item.assert_before_height)
1136	        assert_before_seconds = optional_min(assert_before_seconds, item.assert_before_seconds)

201	        This iterates over the internal items instead of using a query.
202	        """
203	
204	        transaction_ids: list[bytes32] = []
205	
206	        for transaction_id, item in self._items.items():
207	            conds = item.conds
208	            assert conds is not None
209	
210	            for spend in conds.spends:
211	                if spend.coin_id in coin_ids:
212	                    transaction_ids.append(transaction_id)
213	                    break
214	
215	                for puzzle_hash, amount, _memo in spend.create_coin:
216	                    if Coin(spend.coin_id, puzzle_hash, uint64(amount)).name() in coin_ids:
217	                        transaction_ids.append(transaction_id)
218	                        break
219	                else:
220	                    continue
221	
222	                break
223	
224	        return transaction_ids

301	    def get_min_fee_rate(self, cost: int) -> float | None:
302	        """
303	        Gets the minimum fpc rate that a transaction with specified cost will need in order to get included.
304	        """
305	
306	        if not self.at_full_capacity(cost):
307	            return 0
308	
309	        # TODO: make MempoolItem.cost be CLVMCost
310	        current_cost = self._total_cost
311	
312	        # Iterates through all spends in increasing fee per virtual cost
313	        with self._db_conn:
314	            cursor = self._db_conn.execute("SELECT cost,fee_per_cost FROM tx ORDER BY fee_per_cost ASC, seq DESC")
315	
316	            item_cost: int
317	            fee_per_cost: float
318	            for item_cost, fee_per_cost in cursor:
319	                current_cost -= item_cost
320	                # Removing one at a time, until our transaction of size cost fits
321	                if current_cost + cost <= self.mempool_info.max_size_in_cost:
322	                    return fee_per_cost
323	
324	            log.info(
325	                f"Transaction with cost {cost} does not fit in mempool of max cost {self.mempool_info.max_size_in_cost}"
326	            )
327	            return None

329	    def new_tx_block(self, block_height: uint32, timestamp: uint64) -> MempoolRemoveInfo:
330	        """
331	        Remove all items that became invalid because of this new height and
332	        timestamp. (we don't know about which coins were spent in this new block
333	        here, so those are handled separately)
334	        """
335	        with self._db_conn:
336	            cursor = self._db_conn.execute(
337	                "SELECT name FROM tx WHERE assert_before_seconds <= ? OR assert_before_height <= ?",
338	                (timestamp, block_height),
339	            )
340	            to_remove = [bytes32(row[0]) for row in cursor]
341	
342	        self._block_height = block_height
343	        self._timestamp = timestamp
344	
345	        return self.remove_from_pool(to_remove, MempoolRemoveReason.EXPIRED)

347	    def remove_from_pool(self, items: list[bytes32], reason: MempoolRemoveReason) -> MempoolRemoveInfo:
348	        """
349	        Removes an item from the mempool.
350	        """
351	        if items == []:
352	            return MempoolRemoveInfo({}, reason)
353	
354	        removed_items: list[MempoolItemInfo] = []
355	        if reason != MempoolRemoveReason.BLOCK_INCLUSION:
356	            for batch in to_batches(items, SQLITE_MAX_VARIABLE_NUMBER):
357	                args = ",".join(["?"] * len(batch.entries))
358	                with self._db_conn:
359	                    cursor = self._db_conn.execute(
360	                        f"SELECT name, cost, fee FROM tx WHERE name in ({args})", batch.entries
361	                    )
362	                    for row in cursor:
363	                        name = bytes32(row[0])
364	                        internal_item = self._items[name]
365	                        item = MempoolItemInfo(int(row[1]), int(row[2]), internal_item.height_added_to_mempool)
366	                        removed_items.append(item)
367	
368	        removed_internal_items = {name: self._items.pop(name) for name in items}
369	
370	        for batch in to_batches(items, SQLITE_MAX_VARIABLE_NUMBER):
371	            args = ",".join(["?"] * len(batch.entries))
372	            with self._db_conn:
373	                cursor = self._db_conn.execute(
374	                    f"SELECT SUM(cost), SUM(fee) FROM tx WHERE name in ({args})", batch.entries
375	                )
376	                cost_to_remove, fee_to_remove = cursor.fetchone()
377	
378	                self._db_conn.execute(f"DELETE FROM tx WHERE name in ({args})", batch.entries)
379	                self._db_conn.execute(f"DELETE FROM spends WHERE tx in ({args})", batch.entries)
380	
381	            self._total_cost -= cost_to_remove
382	            self._total_fee -= fee_to_remove
383	            assert self._total_cost >= 0
384	            assert self._total_fee >= 0
385	
386	        if reason != MempoolRemoveReason.BLOCK_INCLUSION:
387	            info = FeeMempoolInfo(
388	                self.mempool_info, self.total_mempool_cost(), self.total_mempool_fees(), datetime.now()
389	            )
390	            for iteminfo in removed_items:
391	                self.fee_estimator.remove_mempool_item(info, iteminfo)
392	
393	        return MempoolRemoveInfo(removed_internal_items, reason)
394	
395	    def add_to_pool(self, item: MempoolItem) -> MempoolAddInfo:
396	        """
397	        Adds an item to the mempool by kicking out transactions (if it doesn't fit), in order of increasing fee per cost
398	        """
399	
400	        assert item.fee < MEMPOOL_ITEM_FEE_LIMIT
401	        assert item.conds is not None
402	        assert item.cost <= self.mempool_info.max_block_clvm_cost
403	
404	        removals: list[MempoolRemoveInfo] = []
405	
406	        # we have certain limits on transactions that will expire soon
407	        # (in the next 15 minutes)
408	        block_cutoff = self._block_height + 48
409	        time_cutoff = self._timestamp + 900
410	        if (item.assert_before_height is not None and item.assert_before_height < block_cutoff) or (
411	            item.assert_before_seconds is not None and item.assert_before_seconds < time_cutoff
412	        ):
413	            # this lists only transactions that expire soon, in order of
414	            # lowest fee rate along with the cumulative cost of such
415	            # transactions counting from highest to lowest fee rate
416	            cursor = self._db_conn.execute(
417	                """
418	                SELECT name,
419	                    priority,
420	                    SUM(cost) OVER (ORDER BY priority DESC, seq ASC) AS cumulative_cost
421	                FROM tx
422	                WHERE assert_before_seconds IS NOT NULL AND assert_before_seconds < ?
423	                    OR assert_before_height IS NOT NULL AND assert_before_height < ?
424	                ORDER BY cumulative_cost DESC
425	                """,
426	                (time_cutoff, block_cutoff),
427	            )
428	            to_remove: list[bytes32] = []
429	            for row in cursor:
430	                name, priority, cumulative_cost = row
431	
432	                # there's space for us, stop pruning
433	                if cumulative_cost + item.cost <= self.mempool_info.max_block_clvm_cost:
434	                    break
435	
436	                # we can't evict any more transactions, abort (and don't
437	                # evict what we put aside in "to_remove" list)
438	                if priority > item.fee_per_virtual_cost:
439	                    return MempoolAddInfo([], Err.INVALID_FEE_LOW_FEE)
440	                to_remove.append(name)
441	
442	            removals.append(self.remove_from_pool(to_remove, MempoolRemoveReason.EXPIRED))
443	
444	            # if we don't find any entries, it's OK to add this entry
445	
446	        if self._total_cost + item.cost > self.mempool_info.max_size_in_cost:
447	            # pick the items with the lowest priority to remove
448	            cursor = self._db_conn.execute(
449	                """SELECT name FROM tx
450	                WHERE name NOT IN (
451	                    SELECT name FROM (
452	                        SELECT name,
453	                        SUM(cost) OVER (ORDER BY priority DESC, seq ASC) AS total_cost
454	                        FROM tx) AS tx_with_cost
455	                    WHERE total_cost <= ?)
456	                """,
457	                (self.mempool_info.max_size_in_cost - item.cost,),
458	            )
459	            to_remove = [bytes32(row[0]) for row in cursor]
460	            removals.append(self.remove_from_pool(to_remove, MempoolRemoveReason.POOL_FULL))
461	
462	        with self._db_conn as conn:
463	            # TODO: In the future, for the "fee_per_cost" field, opt for
464	            # "GENERATED ALWAYS AS (CAST(fee AS REAL) / cost) VIRTUAL"
465	            conn.execute(
466	                "INSERT INTO "
467	                "tx(name,cost,fee,assert_height,assert_before_height,assert_before_seconds,"
468	                "fee_per_cost,priority) "
469	                "VALUES(?, ?, ?, ?, ?, ?, ?, ?)",
470	                (
471	                    item.name,
472	                    item.cost,
473	                    item.fee,
474	                    item.assert_height,
475	                    item.assert_before_height,
476	                    item.assert_before_seconds,
477	                    item.fee_per_cost,
478	                    item.fee_per_virtual_cost,
479	                ),
480	            )
481	            all_coin_spends = []
482	            # item.name is a property
483	            # only compute its name once (the spend bundle name)
484	            item_name = item.name
485	            for coin_id, bcs in item.bundle_coin_spends.items():
486	                # any FF spend should be indexed by its latest singleton coin
487	                # ID, this way we'll find it when the singleton is spent
488	                if bcs.latest_singleton_lineage is not None:
489	                    all_coin_spends.append((bcs.latest_singleton_lineage.coin_id, item_name))
490	                else:
491	                    all_coin_spends.append((coin_id, item_name))
492	            conn.executemany("INSERT OR IGNORE INTO spends VALUES(?, ?)", all_coin_spends)
493	
494	        self._items[item_name] = InternalMempoolItem(
495	            item.aggregated_signature, item.conds, item.height_added_to_mempool, item.bundle_coin_spends
496	        )
497	        self._total_cost += item.cost
498	        self._total_fee += item.fee
499	
500	        info = FeeMempoolInfo(self.mempool_info, self.total_mempool_cost(), self.total_mempool_fees(), datetime.now())
501	        self.fee_estimator.add_mempool_item(info, MempoolItemInfo(item.cost, item.fee, item.height_added_to_mempool))
502	        return MempoolAddInfo(removals, None)
503	
504	    # each tuple holds new_coin_id, current_coin_id, mempool item name
505	    def update_spend_index(self, spends_to_update: list[tuple[bytes32, bytes32, bytes32]]) -> None:
506	        with self._db_conn as conn:
507	            conn.executemany("UPDATE OR REPLACE spends SET coin_id=? WHERE coin_id=? AND tx=?", spends_to_update)

509	    def at_full_capacity(self, cost: int) -> bool:
510	        """
511	        Checks whether the mempool is at full capacity and cannot accept a transaction with size cost.
512	        """
513	
514	        return self._total_cost + cost > self.mempool_info.max_size_in_cost

516	    def create_block_generator(
517	        self,
518	        constants: ConsensusConstants,
519	        prev_tx_height: uint32,
520	        timeout: float,
521	    ) -> NewBlockGenerator | None:
522	        """
523	        prev_tx_height is needed in case we fast-forward a transaction and we
524	        need to re-run its puzzle.
525	        """
526	
527	        mempool_bundle = self.create_bundle_from_mempool_items(constants, prev_tx_height, timeout)
528	        if mempool_bundle is None:
529	            return None
530	
531	        spend_bundle, additions = mempool_bundle
532	        removals = spend_bundle.removals()
533	        log.info(f"Add rem: {len(additions)} {len(removals)}")
534	
535	        # since the hard fork has activated, block generators are
536	        # allowed to be serialized with CLVM back-references. We can do that
537	        # unconditionally.
538	        start_time = monotonic()
539	        spends = [(cs.coin, bytes(cs.puzzle_reveal), bytes(cs.solution)) for cs in spend_bundle.coin_spends]
540	        block_program = solution_generator_backrefs(spends)
541	
542	        duration = monotonic() - start_time
543	        log.log(
544	            logging.INFO if duration < 1 else logging.WARNING,
545	            f"serializing block generator took {duration:0.2f} seconds "
546	            f"spends: {len(removals)} additions: {len(additions)}",
547	        )
548	
549	        flags = get_flags_for_height_and_constants(prev_tx_height, constants) | DONT_VALIDATE_SIGNATURE
550	
551	        err, err_msg, conds = run_block_generator2(
552	            block_program,
553	            [],
554	            constants.MAX_BLOCK_COST_CLVM,
555	            flags,
556	            spend_bundle.aggregated_signature,
557	            None,
558	            constants,
559	        )
560	
561	        # this should not happen. This is essentially an assertion failure
562	        if err is not None:  # pragma: no cover
563	            log.error(
564	                f"Failed to compute block cost during farming: {err} {err_msg} "
565	                f"prev-tx-height: {prev_tx_height} "
566	                f"generator: {bytes(block_program).hex()}"
567	            )
568	            return None
569	
570	        assert conds is not None
571	        assert conds.cost > 0
572	
573	        return NewBlockGenerator(
574	            SerializedProgram.from_bytes(block_program),
575	            [],
576	            [],
577	            spend_bundle.aggregated_signature,
578	            additions,
579	            removals,
580	            uint64(conds.cost),
581	        )

583	    def create_bundle_from_mempool_items(
584	        self, constants: ConsensusConstants, prev_tx_height: uint32, timeout: float = 1.0
585	    ) -> tuple[SpendBundle, list[Coin]] | None:
586	        cost_sum = 0  # Checks that total cost does not exceed block maximum
587	        fee_sum = 0  # Checks that total fees don't exceed 64 bits
588	        spend_count = 0  # Checks that total spends do not exceed MAX_SPENDS_PER_BLOCK
589	        processed_spend_bundles = 0
590	        additions: list[Coin] = []
591	        # This contains a map of coin ID to a coin spend solution and its
592	        # isolated cost. We reconstruct it for every bundle we create from
593	        # mempool items because we deduplicate on the first coin spend solution
594	        # that comes with the highest fee rate item, and that can change across
595	        # calls.
596	        dedup_coin_spends = IdenticalSpendDedup()
597	        # This contains a map of fast forward eligible singleton puzzle hash to
598	        # the most recent unspent singleton data, to allow chaining fast forward
599	        # singleton spends.
600	        singleton_ff = SingletonFastForward()
601	        coin_spends: list[CoinSpend] = []
602	        sigs: list[G2Element] = []
603	        log.info(f"Starting to make block, max cost: {self.mempool_info.max_block_clvm_cost}")
604	        bundle_creation_start = monotonic()
605	        cursor = self._db_conn.execute("SELECT name, fee FROM tx ORDER BY priority DESC, seq ASC")
606	        skipped_items = 0
607	        for row in cursor:
608	            name = bytes32(row[0])
609	            fee = int(row[1])
610	            item = self._items[name]
611	
612	            current_time = monotonic()
613	            if current_time - bundle_creation_start >= timeout:
614	                log.info(f"exiting early, already spent {current_time - bundle_creation_start:0.2f} s")
615	                break
616	            try:
617	                assert item.conds is not None
618	                cost = item.conds.cost
619	                if skipped_items >= PRIORITY_TX_THRESHOLD:
620	                    # If we've encountered `PRIORITY_TX_THRESHOLD` number of
621	                    # transactions that don't fit in the remaining block size,
622	                    # we want to keep looking for smaller transactions that
623	                    # might fit, but we also want to avoid spending too much
624	                    # time on potentially expensive ones, hence this shortcut.
625	                    if any(
626	                        sd.eligible_for_dedup or sd.supports_fast_forward for sd in item.bundle_coin_spends.values()
627	                    ):
628	                        log.info(f"Skipping transaction with dedup or FF spends {name}")
629	                        continue
630	
631	                    unique_coin_spends = []
632	                    unique_additions = []
633	                    for spend_data in item.bundle_coin_spends.values():
634	                        unique_coin_spends.append(spend_data.coin_spend)
635	                        unique_additions.extend(spend_data.additions)
636	                    ff_state_update: dict[bytes32, UnspentLineageInfo] = {}
637	                    cost_saving = 0
638	                else:
639	                    bundle_coin_spends, ff_state_update = singleton_ff.process_fast_forward_spends(
640	                        mempool_item=item, prev_tx_height=prev_tx_height, constants=constants
641	                    )
642	                    unique_coin_spends, cost_saving, unique_additions = dedup_coin_spends.get_deduplication_info(
643	                        bundle_coin_spends=bundle_coin_spends
644	                    )
645	                item_cost = cost - cost_saving
646	                log.info(
647	                    "Cumulative cost: %d, fee per cost: %0.4f, item cost: %d", cost_sum, fee / item_cost, item_cost
648	                )
649	                new_fee_sum = fee_sum + fee
650	                if new_fee_sum > DEFAULT_CONSTANTS.MAX_COIN_AMOUNT:
651	                    # Such a fee is very unlikely to happen but we're defensively
652	                    # accounting for it
653	                    break  # pragma: no cover
654	                new_cost_sum = cost_sum + item_cost
655	                new_spend_count = spend_count + len(unique_coin_spends)
656	                if new_cost_sum > self.mempool_info.max_block_clvm_cost or new_spend_count > MAX_SPENDS_PER_BLOCK:
657	                    log.info(
658	                        "Skipping mempool item. Cumulative cost %d (max %d) spends %d (max %d)",
659	                        new_cost_sum,
660	                        self.mempool_info.max_block_clvm_cost,
661	                        new_spend_count,
662	                        MAX_SPENDS_PER_BLOCK,
663	                    )
664	                    skipped_items += 1
665	                    if skipped_items < MAX_SKIPPED_ITEMS:
666	                        continue
667	                    break
668	                singleton_ff.update_fast_forward_spends(ff_state_update)
669	                coin_spends.extend(unique_coin_spends)
670	                additions.extend(unique_additions)
671	                sigs.append(item.aggregated_signature)
672	                cost_sum = new_cost_sum
673	                fee_sum = new_fee_sum
674	                spend_count = new_spend_count
675	                processed_spend_bundles += 1
676	                # Let's stop taking more items if we don't have enough cost left
677	                # for at least `MIN_COST_THRESHOLD` because that would mean we're
678	                # getting very close to the limit anyway and *probably* won't
679	                # find transactions small enough to fit at this point
680	                if (
681	                    self.mempool_info.max_block_clvm_cost - cost_sum < MIN_COST_THRESHOLD
682	                    or spend_count >= MAX_SPENDS_PER_BLOCK
683	                ):
684	                    break
685	            except SkipDedup as e:
686	                log.info(f"{e}")
687	                continue
688	            except Exception as e:
689	                log.info(f"Exception while checking a mempool item for deduplication: {e}")
690	                skipped_items += 1
691	                continue
692	        if coin_spends == []:
693	            return None
694	        log.info(
695	            f"Cumulative cost of block (real cost should be less) {cost_sum}. Proportion "
696	            f"full: {cost_sum / self.mempool_info.max_block_clvm_cost}"
697	        )
698	        aggregated_signature = AugSchemeMPL.aggregate(sigs)
699	        agg = SpendBundle(coin_spends, aggregated_signature)
700	        bundle_creation_end = monotonic()
701	        duration = bundle_creation_end - bundle_creation_start
702	        log.log(
703	            logging.INFO if duration < 1 else logging.WARNING,
704	            f"create_bundle_from_mempool_items took {duration:0.4f} seconds",
705	        )
706	        return agg, additions
707	
708	    def create_block_generator2(
709	        self, constants: ConsensusConstants, prev_tx_height: uint32, timeout: float
710	    ) -> NewBlockGenerator | None:
711	        fee_sum = 0  # Checks that total fees don't exceed 64 bits
712	        additions: list[Coin] = []
713	        removals: list[Coin] = []
714	
715	        dedup_coin_spends = IdenticalSpendDedup()
716	        singleton_ff = SingletonFastForward()
717	        # Fast forward state committed so far from accepted batches, used to
718	        # rollback on batch rejection.
719	        committed_ff = singleton_ff.copy()
720	        log.info(f"Starting to make block, max cost: {self.mempool_info.max_block_clvm_cost}")
721	        generator_creation_start = monotonic()
722	        cursor = self._db_conn.execute("SELECT name, fee FROM tx ORDER BY priority DESC, seq ASC")
723	        builder = BlockBuilder()
724	        skipped_items = 0
725	        # the total (estimated) cost of the transactions added so far
726	        block_cost = 0
727	        added_spends = 0
728	
729	        batch_transactions: list[SpendBundle] = []
730	        batch_additions: list[Coin] = []
731	        batch_spends = 0
732	        # this cost only includes conditions and execution cost, not byte-cost
733	        batch_cost = 0
734	
735	        for row in cursor:
736	            current_time = monotonic()
737	            if current_time - generator_creation_start >= timeout:
738	                log.info(f"exiting early, already spent {current_time - generator_creation_start:0.2f} s")
739	                break
740	
741	            name = bytes32(row[0])
742	            fee = int(row[1])
743	            item = self._items[name]
744	            try:
745	                assert item.conds is not None
746	                cost = item.conds.condition_cost + item.conds.execution_cost
747	                # This `ff_state_update` is only committed later on via
748	                # `update_fast_forward_spends` if the item gets batched.

97	        self.peak_height = int(block.height)
98	        self.peak_hash = header_hash
99	
100	    def include_reward_coins(self, block: FullBlock) -> None:
101	        """Updates `self` with `block`'s reward coins."""
102	        for coin in block.get_included_reward_coins():
103	            assert block.foliage_transaction_block is not None
104	            timestamp = block.foliage_transaction_block.timestamp
105	            coin_id = coin.name()
106	            assert coin_id not in self.additions_since_fork
107	            self.additions_since_fork[coin_id] = ForkAdd(
108	                coin, block.height, timestamp, hint=None, is_coinbase=True, same_as_parent=False
109	            )
110	
111	    def include_spends(self, conds: SpendBundleConditions | None, block: FullBlock, header_hash: bytes32) -> None:
112	        self.update_fork_peak(block, header_hash)
113	        if conds is not None:
114	            assert block.foliage_transaction_block is not None
115	            timestamp = block.foliage_transaction_block.timestamp
116	            for spend in conds.spends:
117	                spend_coin_id = bytes32(spend.coin_id)
118	                self.removals_since_fork[spend_coin_id] = ForkRem(spend.puzzle_hash, block.height)
119	                for puzzle_hash, amount, hint in spend.create_coin:
120	                    coin = Coin(spend_coin_id, puzzle_hash, uint64(amount))
121	                    same_as_parent = coin.puzzle_hash == spend.puzzle_hash and amount == spend.coin_amount
122	                    self.additions_since_fork[coin.name()] = ForkAdd(
123	                        coin, block.height, timestamp, hint=hint, is_coinbase=False, same_as_parent=same_as_parent
124	                    )
125	        self.include_reward_coins(block)
126	
127	    def include_block(
128	        self,
129	        additions: list[tuple[Coin, bytes | None]],
130	        removals: list[tuple[bytes32, Coin]],
131	        block: FullBlock,
132	        header_hash: bytes32,
133	    ) -> None:
134	        self.update_fork_peak(block, header_hash)
135	        if block.foliage_transaction_block is not None:
136	            timestamp = block.foliage_transaction_block.timestamp
137	            spent_coins: dict[bytes32, Coin] = {}
138	            for spend_id, spend in removals:
139	                spent_coins[spend_id] = spend
140	                self.removals_since_fork[spend_id] = ForkRem(spend.puzzle_hash, block.height)
141	            for coin, hint in additions:
142	                parent = spent_coins.get(coin.parent_coin_info)
143	                assert parent is not None
144	                same_as_parent = coin.puzzle_hash == parent.puzzle_hash and coin.amount == parent.amount
145	                self.additions_since_fork[coin.name()] = ForkAdd(
146	                    coin, block.height, timestamp, hint=hint, is_coinbase=False, same_as_parent=same_as_parent
147	                )
148	        self.include_reward_coins(block)
149	
150	    def rollback(self, header_hash: bytes32, height: int) -> None:
151	        assert height <= self.peak_height
152	        self.peak_height = height
153	        self.peak_hash = header_hash
154	        self.additions_since_fork = {k: v for k, v in self.additions_since_fork.items() if v.confirmed_height <= height}
155	        self.removals_since_fork = {k: v for k, v in self.removals_since_fork.items() if v.height <= height}
156	
157	
158	def validate_block_merkle_roots(
159	    block_additions_root: bytes32,
160	    block_removals_root: bytes32,
161	    tx_additions: list[tuple[Coin, bytes32]],
162	    tx_removals: list[bytes32],
163	) -> Err | None:
164	    # Create addition Merkle set
165	    puzzlehash_coins_map: dict[bytes32, list[bytes32]] = {}
166	
167	    for coin, coin_name in tx_additions:
168	        if coin.puzzle_hash in puzzlehash_coins_map:
169	            puzzlehash_coins_map[coin.puzzle_hash].append(coin_name)
170	        else:
171	            puzzlehash_coins_map[coin.puzzle_hash] = [coin_name]
172	
173	    # Addition Merkle set contains puzzlehash and hash of all coins with that puzzlehash
174	    additions_merkle_items: list[bytes32] = []
175	    for puzzle, coin_ids in puzzlehash_coins_map.items():
176	        additions_merkle_items.append(puzzle)
177	        additions_merkle_items.append(hash_coin_ids(coin_ids))
178	
179	    additions_root = bytes32(compute_merkle_set_root(additions_merkle_items))
180	    removals_root = bytes32(compute_merkle_set_root(tx_removals))
181	
182	    if block_additions_root != additions_root:
183	        return Err.BAD_ADDITION_ROOT
184	    if block_removals_root != removals_root:
185	        return Err.BAD_REMOVAL_ROOT
186	
187	    return None
188	
189	
190	async def validate_block_body(
191	    constants: ConsensusConstants,
192	    records: BlockRecordsProtocol,
193	    get_coin_records: Callable[[Collection[bytes32]], Awaitable[list[CoinRecord]]],
194	    block: FullBlock | UnfinishedBlock,
195	    height: uint32,
196	    conds: SpendBundleConditions | None,
197	    fork_info: ForkInfo,
198	    *,
199	    log_coins: bool = False,
200	) -> Err | None:
201	    """
202	    This assumes the header block has been completely validated.
203	    Validates the transactions and body of the block.
204	    Returns None if everything validates correctly, or an Err if something does
205	        not validate.
206	    conds is the result of running the generator with the previous generators
207	        refs. It must be set for transaction blocks and must be None for
208	        non-transaction blocks.
209	    fork_info specifies the fork context of this block. In case the block
210	        extends the main chain, it can be empty, but if the block extends a fork
211	        of the main chain, the fork info is mandatory in order to validate the block.
212	    """
213	    if isinstance(block, FullBlock):
214	        assert height == block.height
215	    prev_transaction_block_height: uint32 = uint32(0)
216	    prev_transaction_block_timestamp: uint64 = uint64(0)
217	
218	    # 1. For non transaction-blocs: foliage block, transaction filter, transactions info, and generator must
219	    # be empty. If it is a block but not a transaction block, there is no body to validate. Check that all fields are
220	    # None
221	    if block.foliage.foliage_transaction_block_hash is None:
222	        if (
223	            block.foliage_transaction_block is not None
224	            or block.transactions_info is not None
225	            or block.transactions_generator is not None
226	        ):
227	            return Err.NOT_BLOCK_BUT_HAS_DATA
228	
229	        prev_tb: BlockRecord | None = records.block_record(block.prev_header_hash)
230	        assert prev_tb is not None
231	        while not prev_tb.is_transaction_block:
232	            prev_tb = records.block_record(prev_tb.prev_hash)
233	            assert prev_tb is not None
234	        assert prev_tb.timestamp is not None
235	        if len(block.transactions_generator_ref_list) > 0:
236	            return Err.NOT_BLOCK_BUT_HAS_DATA
237	
238	        assert fork_info.peak_height == height - 1
239	
240	        assert conds is None
241	        # This means the block is valid
242	        return None
243	
244	    # All checks below this point correspond to transaction blocks
245	    # 2. For blocks, foliage block, transactions info must not be empty
246	    if block.foliage_transaction_block is None or block.transactions_info is None:
247	        return Err.IS_TRANSACTION_BLOCK_BUT_NO_DATA
248	    assert block.foliage_transaction_block is not None
249	
250	    # keeps track of the reward coins that need to be incorporated
251	    expected_reward_coins: set[Coin] = set()
252	
253	    # 3. The transaction info hash in the Foliage block must match the transaction info
254	    if block.foliage_transaction_block.transactions_info_hash != std_hash(block.transactions_info):
255	        return Err.INVALID_TRANSACTIONS_INFO_HASH
256	
257	    # 4. The foliage block hash in the foliage block must match the foliage block
258	    if block.foliage.foliage_transaction_block_hash != std_hash(block.foliage_transaction_block):
259	        return Err.INVALID_FOLIAGE_BLOCK_HASH
260	
261	    # 5. The reward claims must be valid for the previous blocks, and current block fees
262	    # If height == 0, expected_reward_coins will be left empty
263	    if height > 0:
264	        # Add reward claims for all blocks from the prev prev block, until the prev block (including the latter)
265	        prev_transaction_block = records.block_record(block.foliage_transaction_block.prev_transaction_block_hash)
266	        assert prev_transaction_block is not None
267	        prev_transaction_block_height = prev_transaction_block.height
268	        assert prev_transaction_block.timestamp
269	        prev_transaction_block_timestamp = prev_transaction_block.timestamp
270	        assert prev_transaction_block.fees is not None
271	        pool_coin = create_pool_coin(
272	            prev_transaction_block_height,
273	            prev_transaction_block.pool_puzzle_hash,
274	            calculate_pool_reward(prev_transaction_block.height),
275	            constants.GENESIS_CHALLENGE,
276	        )
277	        farmer_coin = create_farmer_coin(
278	            prev_transaction_block_height,
279	            prev_transaction_block.farmer_puzzle_hash,
280	            uint64(calculate_base_farmer_reward(prev_transaction_block.height) + prev_transaction_block.fees),
281	            constants.GENESIS_CHALLENGE,
282	        )
283	        # Adds the previous block
284	        expected_reward_coins.add(pool_coin)
285	        expected_reward_coins.add(farmer_coin)
286	
287	        # For the second block in the chain, don't go back further
288	        if prev_transaction_block.height > 0:
289	            curr_b = records.block_record(prev_transaction_block.prev_hash)
290	            assert curr_b is not None
291	            while not curr_b.is_transaction_block:
292	                expected_reward_coins.add(
293	                    create_pool_coin(
294	                        curr_b.height,
295	                        curr_b.pool_puzzle_hash,
296	                        calculate_pool_reward(curr_b.height),
297	                        constants.GENESIS_CHALLENGE,
298	                    )
299	                )
300	                expected_reward_coins.add(
301	                    create_farmer_coin(
302	                        curr_b.height,
303	                        curr_b.farmer_puzzle_hash,
304	                        calculate_base_farmer_reward(curr_b.height),
305	                        constants.GENESIS_CHALLENGE,
306	                    )
307	                )
308	                curr_b = records.block_record(curr_b.prev_hash)
309	                assert curr_b is not None
310	
311	    if set(block.transactions_info.reward_claims_incorporated) != expected_reward_coins:
312	        return Err.INVALID_REWARD_COINS
313	
314	    if len(block.transactions_info.reward_claims_incorporated) != len(expected_reward_coins):
315	        return Err.INVALID_REWARD_COINS
316	
317	    removals: list[bytes32] = []
318	
319	    # we store coins paired with their names in order to avoid computing the
320	    # coin name multiple times, we store it next to the coin while validating
321	    # the block
322	    coinbase_additions: list[tuple[Coin, bytes32]] = [(c, c.name()) for c in expected_reward_coins]
323	    additions: list[tuple[Coin, bytes32]] = []
324	    removals_puzzle_dic: dict[bytes32, bytes32] = {}
325	    cost: uint64 = uint64(0)
326	
327	    # In header validation we check that timestamp is not more than 5 minutes into the future
328	    # 6. No transactions before INITIAL_TRANSACTION_FREEZE timestamp
329	    # (this test has been removed)
330	
331	    # 7a. The generator root must be the hash of the serialized bytes of
332	    #     the generator for this block (or zeroes if no generator)
333	    if block.transactions_generator is not None:
334	        if std_hash(bytes(block.transactions_generator)) != block.transactions_info.generator_root:
335	            return Err.INVALID_TRANSACTIONS_GENERATOR_HASH
336	    elif block.transactions_info.generator_root != bytes([0] * 32):
337	        return Err.INVALID_TRANSACTIONS_GENERATOR_HASH
338	
339	    # 8a. The generator_ref_list must be the hash of the serialized bytes of
340	    #     the generator ref list for this block (or 'one' bytes [0x01] if no generator)
341	    # 8b. The generator ref list length must be less than or equal to MAX_GENERATOR_REF_LIST_SIZE entries
342	    # 8c. The generator ref list must not point to a height >= this block's height
343	    if block.transactions_generator_ref_list == []:
344	        if block.transactions_info.generator_refs_root != bytes([1] * 32):
345	            return Err.INVALID_TRANSACTIONS_GENERATOR_REFS_ROOT
346	    else:
347	        # With hard fork 2 we ban transactions_generator_ref_list.
348	        if prev_transaction_block_height >= constants.SOFT_FORK9_HEIGHT:
349	            return Err.TOO_MANY_GENERATOR_REFS
350	
351	        # If we have a generator reference list, we must have a generator
352	        if block.transactions_generator is None:
353	            return Err.INVALID_TRANSACTIONS_GENERATOR_REFS_ROOT
354	
355	        # The generator_refs_root must be the hash of the concatenation of the list[uint32]
356	        generator_refs_hash = std_hash(b"".join([i.stream_to_bytes() for i in block.transactions_generator_ref_list]))
357	        if block.transactions_info.generator_refs_root != generator_refs_hash:
358	            return Err.INVALID_TRANSACTIONS_GENERATOR_REFS_ROOT
359	        if len(block.transactions_generator_ref_list) > constants.MAX_GENERATOR_REF_LIST_SIZE:
360	            return Err.TOO_MANY_GENERATOR_REFS
361	        if any([index >= height for index in block.transactions_generator_ref_list]):
362	            return Err.FUTURE_GENERATOR_REFS
363	
364	    if block.transactions_generator is not None:
365	        # Get List of names removed, puzzles hashes for removed coins and conditions created
366	
367	        cost = uint64(0 if conds is None else conds.cost)
368	
369	        # 7. Check that cost <= MAX_BLOCK_COST_CLVM
370	        log.debug(
371	            f"Cost: {cost} max: {constants.MAX_BLOCK_COST_CLVM} "
372	            f"percent full: {round(100 * (cost / constants.MAX_BLOCK_COST_CLVM), 2)}%"
373	        )
374	        if cost > constants.MAX_BLOCK_COST_CLVM:
375	            return Err.BLOCK_COST_EXCEEDS_MAX
376	
377	        # 8. The CLVM program must not return any errors
378	        assert conds is not None
379	        assert conds.validated_signature
380	
381	        if prev_transaction_block_height >= constants.SOFT_FORK9_HEIGHT:
382	            if not is_canonical_serialization(bytes(block.transactions_generator)):
383	                return Err.INVALID_TRANSACTIONS_GENERATOR_ENCODING
384	
385	        for spend in conds.spends:
386	            removals.append(bytes32(spend.coin_id))
387	            removals_puzzle_dic[spend.coin_id] = spend.puzzle_hash
388	            for puzzle_hash, amount, _ in spend.create_coin:
389	                c = Coin(spend.coin_id, puzzle_hash, uint64(amount))
390	                additions.append((c, c.name()))
391	    else:
392	        assert conds is None
393	
394	    # 9. Check that the correct cost is in the transactions info
395	    if block.transactions_info.cost != cost:
396	        return Err.INVALID_BLOCK_COST
397	
398	    additions_dic: dict[bytes32, Coin] = {}
399	    # 10. Check additions for max coin amount
400	    # Be careful to check for 64 bit overflows in other languages. This is the max 64 bit unsigned integer
401	    # We will not even reach here because Coins do type checking (uint64)
402	    for coin, coin_name in additions + coinbase_additions:
403	        additions_dic[coin_name] = coin
404	        if coin.amount < 0:
405	            return Err.COIN_AMOUNT_NEGATIVE
406	
407	        if coin.amount > constants.MAX_COIN_AMOUNT:
408	            return Err.COIN_AMOUNT_EXCEEDS_MAXIMUM
409	
410	    # 11. Validate addition and removal roots
411	    root_error = validate_block_merkle_roots(
412	        block.foliage_transaction_block.additions_root,
413	        block.foliage_transaction_block.removals_root,
414	        additions + coinbase_additions,
415	        removals,
416	    )
417	    if root_error is not None:
418	        return root_error
419	
420	    # 12. The additions and removals must result in the correct filter
421	    byte_array_tx: list[bytearray] = []
422	
423	    for coin, _ in additions + coinbase_additions:
424	        byte_array_tx.append(bytearray(coin.puzzle_hash))
425	    for coin_name in removals:
426	        byte_array_tx.append(bytearray(coin_name))
427	
428	    bip158: PyBIP158 = PyBIP158(byte_array_tx)
429	    encoded_filter = bytes(bip158.GetEncoded())
430	    filter_hash = std_hash(encoded_filter)
431	
432	    if filter_hash != block.foliage_transaction_block.filter_hash:
433	        return Err.INVALID_TRANSACTIONS_FILTER_HASH
434	
435	    # 13. Check for duplicate outputs in additions
436	    addition_counter = collections.Counter(coin_name for _, coin_name in additions + coinbase_additions)
437	    for count in addition_counter.values():
438	        if count > 1:
439	            return Err.DUPLICATE_OUTPUT
440	
441	    # 14. Check for duplicate spends inside block
442	    removal_counter = collections.Counter(removals)
443	    for count in removal_counter.values():
444	        if count > 1:
445	            return Err.DOUBLE_SPEND
446	
447	    # 15. Check if removals exist and were not previously spent. (unspent_db + diff_store + this_block)
448	    # The fork point is the last block in common between the peak chain and the chain of `block`
449	
450	    assert fork_info.fork_height < height
451	    assert fork_info.peak_height == height - 1
452	
453	    removal_coin_records: dict[bytes32, CoinRecord] = {}
454	    # the removed coins we need to look up from the DB
455	    # i.e. all non-ephemeral coins
456	    removals_from_db: list[bytes32] = []
457	    for rem in removals:
458	        if rem in additions_dic:
459	            # Ephemeral coin
460	            rem_coin: Coin = additions_dic[rem]
461	            new_unspent: CoinRecord = CoinRecord(
462	                rem_coin,
463	                height,
464	                height,
465	                False,
466	                block.foliage_transaction_block.timestamp,
467	            )
468	            removal_coin_records[new_unspent.name] = new_unspent
469	        else:
470	            # This check applies to both coins created before fork (pulled from coin_store),
471	            # and coins created after fork (additions_since_fork)
472	            if rem in fork_info.removals_since_fork:
473	                # This coin was spent in the fork
474	                log.error(f"Err.DOUBLE_SPEND_IN_FORK {fork_info.removals_since_fork[rem]}")
475	                return Err.DOUBLE_SPEND_IN_FORK
476	            removals_from_db.append(rem)
477	
478	    unspent_records = await get_coin_records(removals_from_db)
479	
480	    # some coin spends we need to ensure exist in the fork branch. Both coins we
481	    # can't find in the DB, but also coins that were spent after the fork point
482	    look_in_fork: list[bytes32] = []
483	    for unspent in unspent_records:
484	        if unspent.confirmed_block_index <= fork_info.fork_height:
485	            # Spending something in the current chain, confirmed before fork
486	            # (We ignore all coins confirmed after fork)
487	            if unspent.spent == 1 and unspent.spent_block_index <= fork_info.fork_height:
488	                # Check for coins spent in an ancestor block
489	                return Err.DOUBLE_SPEND
490	            removal_coin_records[unspent.name] = unspent
491	        else:
492	            look_in_fork.append(unspent.name)
493	
494	    if log_coins and len(look_in_fork) > 0:
495	        log.info("%d coins spent after fork", len(look_in_fork))
496	
497	    if len(unspent_records) != len(removals_from_db):
498	        # some coins could not be found in the DB. We need to find out which
499	        # ones and look for them in additions_since_fork
500	        found: set[bytes32] = {u.name for u in unspent_records}
501	        for rem in removals_from_db:
502	            if rem in found:
503	                continue
504	            look_in_fork.append(rem)
505	
506	    if log_coins and len(look_in_fork) > 0:
507	        log.info("coins spent in fork: %s", ",".join([f"{name}"[0:6] for name in look_in_fork]))
508	
509	    for rem in look_in_fork:
510	        # This coin is not in the current heaviest chain, so it must be in the fork
511	        if rem not in fork_info.additions_since_fork:
512	            # Check for spending a coin that does not exist in this fork
513	            log.error(f"Err.UNKNOWN_UNSPENT: COIN ID: {rem} fork_info: {fork_info}")
514	            return Err.UNKNOWN_UNSPENT
515	        addition: ForkAdd = fork_info.additions_since_fork[rem]
516	        new_coin_record: CoinRecord = CoinRecord(
517	            addition.coin,
518	            addition.confirmed_height,
519	            uint32(0),
520	            False,
521	            addition.timestamp,
522	        )
523	        removal_coin_records[new_coin_record.name] = new_coin_record
524	
525	    removed = 0
526	    for unspent in removal_coin_records.values():
527	        removed += unspent.coin.amount
528	
529	    added = 0
530	    for coin, _ in additions:
531	        added += coin.amount
532	
533	    # 16. Check that the total coin amount for added is <= removed
534	    if removed < added:
535	        return Err.MINTING_COIN
536	
537	    fees = removed - added
538	    assert fees >= 0
539	
540	    # reserve fee cannot be greater than UINT64_MAX per consensus rule.
541	    # run_generator() would fail
542	    assert_fee_sum = uint64(0 if conds is None else conds.reserve_fee)
543	
544	    # 17. Check that the assert fee sum <= fees, and that each reserved fee is non-negative
545	    if fees < assert_fee_sum:
546	        return Err.RESERVE_FEE_CONDITION_FAILED
547	
548	    # 18. Check that the fee amount + farmer reward < maximum coin amount
549	    if fees + calculate_base_farmer_reward(height) > constants.MAX_COIN_AMOUNT:
550	        return Err.COIN_AMOUNT_EXCEEDS_MAXIMUM
551	
552	    # 19. Check that the computed fees are equal to the fees in the block header
553	    if block.transactions_info.fees != fees:
554	        return Err.INVALID_BLOCK_FEE_AMOUNT
555	
556	    # 20. Verify that removed coin puzzle_hashes match with calculated puzzle_hashes
557	    for unspent in removal_coin_records.values():
558	        if unspent.coin.puzzle_hash != removals_puzzle_dic[unspent.name]:
559	            return Err.WRONG_PUZZLE_HASH
560	
561	    # 21. Verify conditions
562	    # verify absolute/relative height/time conditions
563	    if conds is not None:
564	        error: int | None = check_time_locks(
565	            removal_coin_records,
566	            conds,
567	            prev_transaction_block_height,
568	            prev_transaction_block_timestamp,
569	            nowrap=(prev_transaction_block_height >= constants.HARD_FORK2_HEIGHT),
570	        )
571	        if error is not None:
572	            # TODO: standardise errors across Rust and Python so cast is not necesary here
573	            return Err(error)
574	
575	    # 22. Verify aggregated signature is done in pre-validation
576	    if not block.transactions_info.aggregated_signature:
577	        return Err.BAD_AGGREGATE_SIGNATURE
578	
579	    return None
